case_title: IRENE HSIEH VS. SOLAR POWER INC. et al

docket_entries.description: LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS SOLAR POWER INC., STEPHEN KIRCHER AND JAMES PEKARSKY'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS GRANTED AS TO THE PRAYER FOR ATTORNEYS FEES. OTHERWISE, OFF CALENDAR IN LIGHT OF THE RULING ON THE DEMURRER, WITH THE EXPECTATION THAT ANY AMENDED COMPLAINT WILL NOT INCLUDE ALLEGATIONS WITHOUT ANY RELEVANCE TO THE CLAIMS STATED IN THAT AMENDED PLEADING, AND WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO THE MOTION TO STRIKE BEING RENEWED IF APPROPRIATE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS SOLAR POWER INC., STEPHEN KIRCHER & JAMES PEKARSKY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE RETALIATION CLAIM AGAINST THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS. OTHERWISE, SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD FACTS AND A VIABLE LEGAL THEORY OF ACTIONABLE RETALIATION AND TO PLEAD FACTS SUFFICIENT TO SUPPORT A DEFAMATION CLAIM. DISCOVERY, 302, NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF IRENE HSIEH'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER DISCOVERY RESPONSES, FOR ADVERSE INFERENCE, APPOINTMENT OF FORENSIC EXPERT, AND SANCTIONS IS DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY. INASMUCH AS THE DEFAMATION CLAIM HAS BEEN ADJUDICATED IN FAVOR OF THE DEFENDANTS, THOSE REQUESTS ARE MOOT. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN THAT THERE HAS BEEN A SPECIFIC LACK OF COMPLIANCE WITH THE PRIOR DISCOVERY ORDER ON SEPTEMBER 5, 2013. IN FACT, IT WOULD APPEAR THAT PLAINTIFF'S REFUSAL TO MORE NARROWLY TAILOR THEIR REQUESTS PREDICTABLY RESULTED IN A LARGE PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR AN ADVERSE INFERENCE AND APPOINTMENT OF FORENSIC EXPERT ARE NOT BRIEFED SUBSTANTIALLY ENOUGH AND ARE BETTER LEFT TO THE TRIAL COURT TO DECIDE. THE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM PETER VAZANDT, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.
DISMISSAL WITH PREJUDICE OF 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT AS TO DEFENDANT PEKARSKY, JAMES R. KIRCHER, STEPHEN C.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SOLAR POWER INC., STEPHEN KIRCHER AND JAMES PEKARSKY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS CONTINUED TO DECEMBER 17, 2013, TO GIVE THE OPPOSING PARTY A CHANCE TO COMPLY WITH SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULE 2.6B. THE OPPOSING PARTY SHALL PROVIDE A COURTESY COPY OF THE DECLARATION OF GAUTAM DUTTA WITH ATTACHMENTS THAT WAS FILED ON DECEMBER 2, 2013. PLEASE ATTACH A COVER LETTER TO THE DOCUMENT THAT REFERENCES THE NEW HEARING DATE. IN ADDITION, THE COURT DID NOT RECEIVE A COURTESY COPY OF THE OPPOSING SEPARATE STATEMENT AND DECLARATION OF IRENE HSIEH THAT WERE FILED ON NOVEMBER 27, 2013. THE COURT, HOWEVER, DID RECEIVE THE REST OF PLAINTIFF'S FILINGS INCLUDING THE CORRECTED DECLARATION OF IRENE HSIEH AND CORRECTED OPPOSING SEPARATE STATEMENT THAT WERE FILED ON DECEMBER 2, 2013. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: LAURA MARTINEZ, CSR #11332

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS SOLAR POWER INC., STEPHEN KIRCHER AND JAMES PEKARSKY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AND DENIED IN PART. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO THE WRONGFUL TERMINATION IN VIOLATION OF PUBLIC POLICY CAUSE OF ACTION. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING WHETHER PLAINTIFF ENGAGED IN PROTECTED ACTIVITY AND WHETHER HER TERMINATION WAS THE RESULT OF THAT ACTIVITY. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO THE DEFAMATION CAUSE OF ACTION. DEFENDANT PRESENTS DECLARATIONS FROM MR. KIRCHER AND MR. PEKARSKY WHO CLEARLY STATE THAT THEY NEVER COMMUNICATED ORALLY OR IN WRITING WITH SOLARCITY. THAT EVIDENCE SHIFTS THE BURDEN OF PRODUCTION AND IN OPPOSITION, PLAINTIFF FAILS TO PRESENT ANY CONFLICTING EVIDENCE. THE COURT DENIES THE REQUEST FOR A CONTINUANCE PER CCP 437C(H). FIRST, PLAINTIFF DID NOT FORMALLY MAKE SUCH A REQUEST IN THE OPPOSITION. SECOND, PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATIONS DO NOT CLEARLY STATE WHAT FACTS PLAINTIFF HOPES TO FIND TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT. THIRD, PLAINTIFF DID NOT ACT DILIGENTLY. THE COURT NOTES THAT THERE WAS NO SHOWING THAT PLAINTIFF SOUGHT TO DEPOSE WITNESSES AT SOLARCITY OR SUBPOENA DOCUMENTS FROM THAT ENTITY. LASTLY, A CONTINUANCE WOULD BE PREJUDICIAL TO DEFENDANT GIVEN THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: LAURA MARTINEZ, CSR #11332

Off Calendar Dec-26-2013 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Feb-04-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Discovery Responses, For Adverse Inference, Appointment Of Forensic Expert, And Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-24-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF IRENE HSIEH's Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses, For Adverse Inference, Appointment Of Forensic Expert, And Sanctions is DENIED in its entirety. Inasmuch as the defamation claim has been adjudicated in favor of the defendants, those requests are moot. Plaintiff has not shown that there has been a specific lack of compliance with the prior discovery Order on September 5, 2013. In fact, it would appear that Plaintiff's refusal to more narrowly tailor their requests predictably resulted in a large production of documents. Plaintiff's request for an adverse inference and appointment of forensic expert are not briefed substantially enough and are better left to the trial court to decide. The request for sanctions is denied. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM PETER VAZANDT, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-27-2013 Off Calendar

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Dec-18-2013 Text Ruling

Defendants Solar Power Inc.'S, Stephen Kircher'S, And James Pekarsky'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Dec-17-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: GAUTAM FUTTA, ESQ., 415.236.2048, FOR PLAINTIFF IRENE HSIEH.
LINDA MORONEY, ESQ., 415.986.5900, FOR DEFENDANTS SOLAR POWER INC., STEPHEN KIRCHER AND JAMES PEKARSKY.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: Defendants Solar Power Inc., Stephen Kircher and James Pekarsky's Motion For Summary Judgment is denied. The motion for summary adjudication is granted and denied in part. The motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the wrongful termination in violation of public policy cause of action. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether plaintiff engaged in protected activity and whether her termination was the result of that activity. The motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the defamation cause of action. Defendant presents declarations from Mr. Kircher and Mr. Pekarsky who clearly state that they never communicated orally or in writing with SolarCity. That evidence shifts the burden of production and in opposition, plaintiff fails to present any conflicting evidence. The Court denies the request for a continuance per CCP 437c(h). First, plaintiff did not formally make such a request in the opposition. Second, plaintiff's declarations do not clearly state what facts plaintiff hopes to find to create a triable issue of material fact. Third, plaintiff did not act diligently. The court notes that there was no showing that plaintiff sought to depose witnesses at SolarCity or subpoena documents from that entity. Lastly, a continuance would be prejudicial to defendant given the current trial date. Order signed in open court. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Laura Martinez, CSR #11332

RULING: NO ARGUMENT. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS SOLAR POWER INC., STEPHEN KIRCHER AND JAMES PEKARSKY'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS Granted as to the prayer for attorneys fees. Otherwise, off calendar in light of the ruling on the demurrer, with the expectation that any amended complaint will not include allegations without any relevance to the claims stated in that amended pleading, and without prejudice to the motion to strike being renewed if appropriate. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-15-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: gautam dutta, esq., 415.236.2048, for plaintiff irene hsieh.
linda moroney, esq., 415.986.5900, for defendants solar power inc., stephen kircher and james pekarsky.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS SOLAR POWER INC., STEPHEN KIRCHER & JAMES PEKARSKY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS Sustained without leave to amend as to the retaliation claim against the individual defendants. Otherwise, sustained with 10 days leave to amend to plead facts and a viable legal theory of actionable retaliation and to plead facts sufficient to support a defamation claim. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
